_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of economic and accounting principles and practices, the financial markets, banking, and the analysis and reporting of financial data.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data monetization models, including direct data licensing, data-as-a-service, embedded analytics, and indirect value creation through operational efficiency, and methods for quantifying data asset value.
comments: O*NET covers broad economics/accounting/finance. WIDAI LS-K-010 covers data monetization specifically. Partial overlap in financial analysis concepts. Secondary: RM-K-001 (risk quantification uses financial concepts), LS-K-008 (operational budgeting). O*NET is much broader.
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Weak similarity (nist band: much more dissimilar than similar). The moderate STS score is consistent with a Superset relationship where the O*NET element covers significantly broader ground than the WIDAI KSA. The shared conceptual territory is real but represents a fraction of the O*NET element's scope, compressing the similarity signal.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: F1 of 0.8668 indicates moderate token-level semantic alignment. Precision exceeds Recall, indicating that the FDE's semantic content is more tightly aligned with the KSA than the reverse — the KSA contains concepts or specificity beyond what the FDE covers. The P > R pattern is atypical for a Superset relationship — it suggests the KSA may contain specificity that extends beyond the FDE's scope. This warrants attention during human review, though it may reflect domain-specific terminology in the KSA that the FDE covers conceptually without matching at the token level.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Neutral-dominant (98.80%) — the model finds the descriptions related but neither implying nor contradicting each other. Neutral is common for Superset pairs where the scope gap is large — the FDE covers broader territory than the KSA, so the NLI model sees topical relatedness without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: The bi-encoder score (0.4589) meets or exceeds the STS cross-encoder (0.3287). This is uncommon and suggests the semantic similarity is well-represented in the embedding space — the descriptions occupy similar vector-space regions even without joint processing.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Low lexical overlap (11.76% of tokens shared). Some shared vocabulary exists (likely common functional words or broadly-used domain terms) but the bulk of each description uses distinct terminology.
